role: Prime Minister of Iran (1951–1953); founder of National Front
bio: Mohammad Mossadegh was Prime Minister of Iran from 1951 to 1953. He founded the [National Front](/orgs/national-front-iran) in 1949 and led the campaign to nationalize Iran's oil industry, previously controlled by the Anglo-Iranian Oil Company. His democratically elected government was overthrown in the August 1953 coup d'état orchestrated by the CIA and MI6 (Operation Ajax/Boot). He was tried by a military tribunal, sentenced to three years in prison, and then placed under house arrest in his estate in Ahmadabad, where he remained until his death on 5 March 1967. His legacy shaped the ideology of the [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran), whose members described themselves as 'Mossadeghists.'
editorial_notes: Born 1882 Tehran. PM 1951-1953. Overthrown August 1953 CIA/MI6 coup. Died 5 March 1967 under house arrest.
